commands.repair_reason: The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof and maintainer validation, not a narrow repair ClawSweeper can safely add on the PR branch.; Cleared: The diff only changes retry classification, Telegram fetch retry handling, and tests; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, package metadata, or code execution surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds HTTP 421/Misdirected Request detection to shared and Telegram strict retry paths, forces Telegram transport fallback before retrying 421 responses/errors, and adds focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main omits 421 from both the shared retry regex and the strict Telegram send predicate, while outbound sends use strictShouldRetry. I did not live-reproduce Telegram's intermittent 421 path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof and maintainer validation, not a narrow repair ClawSweeper can safely add on the PR branch.

target.title: fix(telegram): retry 421 misdirected request responses
target.body:
Summary
- treat Telegram HTTP 421 / "Misdirected Request" responses as retryable transport failures
- wire 421 handling into the strict Telegram outbound send retry path used by non-idempotent send operations
- keep the default channel API retry regex coverage for non-strict retry callers
- add regression coverage for both default retry behavior and the strict send retry predicate
- closes #48892

Change Type
- bug fix

Scope
- src/infra/retry-policy.ts
- src/infra/retry-policy.test.ts
- extensions/telegram/src/network-errors.ts
- extensions/telegram/src/network-errors.test.ts

User-visible / Behavior Changes
- Telegram outbound sends such as sendMessage/media sends can retry transient HTTP 421 Misdirected Request responses instead of failing on the first attempt
- The strict send retry path remains conservative for non-idempotent operations and does not broadly retry ambiguous network errors that could duplicate messages

Review Notes
- Addresses Codex feedback by adding 421 detection to `isSafeToRetrySendError`, which is the predicate used by the strict Telegram send path
- The non-strict channel API retry regex also recognizes HTTP 421 because it is a transport/routing-level misdirection response and is safe to retry in the default retry path
- Tightens string status parsing so malformed values such as `421abc` are not treated as HTTP 421
- Moves the default retry-policy 421 regression case out of the `strictShouldRetry` suite

Test plan
- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
- pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/infra/retry-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/network-errors.test.ts
target.author: marsdoge
